system.description:
Discipline: Waijia
Skill: Grapple against Parry, then Grapple against Hardiness
Type: Normal
Qi: 1

You grip your opponent’s limbs and snap them, like branches clutched in the beak of a hawk. You can attempt this if you succeed on a Grapple Skill roll against the target’s Parry. Follow the grapple immediately with another Grapple roll against Hardiness. On a Success you injure the target’s arm or leg briefly, causing the following penalties for 1 round per Rank of Waijia. Attackers chooses: Arm Injury: -2 Penalty to Parry, -1d10 to Arm Strike, Grapple and cannot use two-handed Melee weapons. Leg Injury: -2d10 Penalty to Speed, -1d10 to Leg Strike, Grapple and Athletics. This ability does not work on characters with Qi levels higher than you. Cathartic: You fracture or break the limb, causing the above penalties for 1 week per Rank of Waijia. This is considered very cruel and even dishonorable to do to a Martial Hero, so performing such an act publicly could alter your reputation. Still does not work against those with higher Qi levels than you.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.targetEffects: Choose arm or leg injury for 1 round per Rank of Waijia
system.catharticTargetEffects: Choose arm or leg fracture for 1 week per Rank of Waijia
system.targetEffectsNote: Arm Injury: Parry -2, Arm Strike and Grapple -1d10, no two-handed melee weapons. Leg Injury: Speed -2d10, Leg Strike/Grapple/Athletics -1d10.
system.catharticEffect: You fracture or break the limb, causing the above penalties for 1 week per Rank of Waijia. This is considered very cruel and even dishonorable to do to a Martial Hero, so performing such an act publicly could alter your reputation. Still does not work against those with higher Qi levels than you.
